codeReview.summary: The migration rewrites the swap widget to Tamagui and preserves swap/limit behavior, routing, wallet flows, and modal interactions in the reviewed diff. Required styling targets in the feature contract are implemented, with no blocking functional regressions identified in the commit.

issues.description: The component includes several hardcoded color literals (e.g., lines 710, 787, 992) instead of theme tokens, which diverges from mission convention and may reduce long-term theming consistency.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill's mandatory TDD step does not fit this class of visual migration work where no swap-widget test harness exists, leading to justified procedural deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md section '2. Write Tests First (TDD)' vs handoff.skillFeedback.deviations[0] explicitly skipping TDD for this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill should document current agent-browser command patterns to avoid trial-and-error during required visual checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton records failed command `agent-browser --session "ea262eb3b7d5" --url "http://localhost:3102" screenshot ...` with `Unknown command: --url` before switching to `open`/`screenshot`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Color-token guidance is absolute in mission AGENTS.md but real component migrations still require raw style literals in places; the convention needs scoped exceptions or explicit fallback guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md states 'ALWAYS use Tamagui theme tokens, never hardcoded hex colors'; this feature uses literals at web/components/swap/swap-widget.tsx lines 710, 787, 992.

summary: Reviewed feature swap-widget-tamagui at commit cc96e77: implementation satisfies the migration intent and preserves behavior, so review passes. Logged one non-blocking convention issue (hardcoded colors) and shared-state observations about skill procedure/documentation gaps.
